title: Under the Hood: How JavaScript Engines Like V8 Work

questions.sentence: To write truly high-performance JavaScript, it is essential to understand how the code is executed 'Under the Hood'.
The most prevalent JavaScript engine today is 'V8', which powers Google Chrome and Node.js.
V8 uses a process called 'Just-In-Time' (JIT) compilation to convert your JavaScript into highly optimized machine code.
During execution, V8 identifies 'Hot' code paths—functions that are called frequently—and optimizes them by making assumptions about the types of data they handle.
By understanding these low-level mechanisms, senior developers can write code that is more 'Engine-friendly', significantly improving the speed and efficiency of complex applications.
